matches.moment: Mbappe releases Olise on the break in the 84th minute as three Norwegian defenders are caught high in France's half — Norway's all-in attacking posture collapses when Olise's clinical finish ends the contest.

Saudi Arabia clear a corner only to the edge of the area in the 67th minute — Garry Rodrigues reads the second ball first, controls and finishes low before the Saudi recovery shape resets, punishing defensive disorganization from a side that pushed every body forward chasing the game.

Yamal's incisive through-ball in the 72nd minute splits Uruguay's defensive line — exposed through the center without Araujo anchoring it — sending Nico Williams clear to slot past Muslera and make the result safe, five minutes before Nunez's consolation.

Mohamed Salah bends in a free kick from 22 yards in the 61st minute — the single piece of individual precision on a night where Iran's defensive block holds for an hour before being undone by the one moment it cannot game-plan for; Marmoush seals it as Iran abandon structure chasing an equalizer.

Kevin De Bruyne's precise free kick from 20 yards in the 64th minute finally gives Belgium the two-goal cushion that breaks New Zealand's resistance — the set-piece precision delivering what open-play service failed to produce across two prior group matches.

verdict: June 26 is the day the 2026 World Cup's group-stage picture finally resolves, and the numbers point to an outcome that rewards quality over desperation across all five matches. In Group I, France and Norway deliver what their form demanded: a competitive, open game at Gillette Stadium where Norway's structural need to win creates the space Mbappe needs to confirm his place at the top of the Golden Boot conversation — 6 goals in 3 games would be the statement of a player who arrived at this World Cup in form that demands serious attention for tournament honors. The Group H double-header settles the bracket cleanly: Spain advance as group winners powered by Yamal's incisiveness through a Uruguay defense that cannot recover from losing both Araujo and de Arrascaeta in the same elimination game; Cape Verde's extraordinary tournament story — a team that held Spain to 0-0 and drew Uruguay 2-2 — gets the final-chapter win it deserves when Saudi Arabia's desperation attacking leaves the spaces a low-block counter-attacking side is built to exploit. Group G delivers the starkest corrective moment of the day: Belgium's catastrophic xG-to-goals underperformance (1.81/game to 0.5/game actual) finally resolves at BC Place, where New Zealand's must-attack posture provides exactly the open space Belgium's attackers have been denied; meanwhile Egypt confirm their group leadership with the Salah free-kick that has been building since this tournament began. The tournament picture after today sees 24 of the 32 Round of 32 places confirmed, with France, Spain, and Belgium from today's slate entering the knockout rounds as three of the four or five genuine title contenders the data consistently identifies. — The Analyst.

players.name: Erling Haaland
players.stat: 4 goals in 2 tournament games (2 vs Iraq, 2 vs Senegal) — joint 2nd in Golden Boot
players.watch: Whether Haaland drops deep to link play and bypass Saliba — his combination with Nusa in tight spaces rather than pure aerial duels vs France's elite backline

players.name: Kylian Mbappé
players.stat: 4 goals in 2 tournament games — tied for Golden Boot lead alongside Haaland and Messi
players.watch: Mbappé in the first 20 minutes when Norway are fresh but already committed to attacking — his first-half counter is France's most efficient weapon

players.name: Lamine Yamal
players.stat: 1 goal in tournament; 18 goals + 25 assists in 2024-25 club season (fallback — club stat); Ballon d'Or winner at 18
players.watch: His through-ball precision targeting the space between Uruguay's CB pairing — specifically the channel opened by Araujo's absence — and whether he completes 90 minutes or is subbed with hamstring precaution

players.name: Mohamed Salah
players.stat: 1 goal + 1 assist in tournament; chasing Hossam Hassan's all-time Egypt goals record
players.watch: Salah's positioning at free kicks 20-22 yards from goal in the second half — if Egypt win a dead ball in that zone, this is his moment against Iran's compact block

players.name: Kevin De Bruyne
players.stat: Belgium 1.81 xG/game in tournament (De Bruyne primary creator); 0.5 actual goals/game — extreme underperformance predicts correction
players.watch: His free-kick delivery in the 60-70 minute window — Belgium's set-piece excellence has been underutilized across two group matches and is the correction mechanism for their xG gap

players.name: Darwin Núñez
players.stat: 13 career international goals (fallback); 0 goals in 2 tournament games vs Saudi Arabia and Cape Verde
players.watch: His 60-80 minute performance when Uruguay are behind and Bielsa commits to all-out attack — the consolation goal window when Spain drop back at 2-0
